section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing
Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents
Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, equipment — not present in Tier 3
Commencing any Phase 8 activity — including preparatory drafting — before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable to artifact type: the audited artifact has schema_id 'orch.phase6.implementation_architecture.v1', identifying it as a Phase 6 Tier 4 output. Check 13.4 applies to Phase 8 artifacts (schema_id beginning 'orch.tier5.' or 'orch.phase8.'). No budget figures (euro amounts, person-month values used as budget-confirmed allocations) are present in the artifact, consistent with Phase 6 preceding the budget gate. Phase 7 budget gate has not yet been evaluated; no Phase 8 activity is evidenced in or triggered by this artifact.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable to artifact-level check: whether durable decisions were held only in agent memory cannot be determined by reading artifact content. This prohibition is enforced by the decision-log-update skill and runtime architecture, not by inspection of the produced artifact.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable to artifact-level check: whether a skill or workflow has become a de facto constitutional authority cannot be determined from reading artifact content alone. This requires inspection of skill and workflow definition files, not the produced phase output.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete
Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable to artifact type: check 13.8 applies to Tier 5 deliverable artifacts. The audited artifact has schema_id 'orch.phase6.implementation_architecture.v1', identifying it as a Tier 4 phase output, not a proposal section or assembled draft. Where partial coverage is acknowledged (instrument_sections_addressed entry for B.3.1 carries status 'partially_addressed' with an explicit explanation that Tables 3.1a–h are Phase 3 and Phase 4 outputs), the incompleteness is explicitly flagged and not silently omitted.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible
section13_checks.violation_evidence: The artifact contains multiple regulatory claims referencing Horizon Europe programme rules — including GEP eligibility obligations (ethics_grounding.gender_plan_grounding), ethics self-assessment requirements (ethics_grounding.self_assessment_required_source), civil-focus constraint applicability (ETH-01.source_basis, ethics_grounding.civil_focus_grounding), grant agreement implementation obligations (governance_matrix[0].source_basis, governance_matrix[1].source_basis, governance_matrix[2].source_basis), and GEP management balance guidance (ethics_grounding.gender_management_balance_grounding) — but the artifact does not contain a traceability_footer field. Consequently, no primary_sources[] array exists pointing to paths within docs/tier1_normative_framework/extracted/ as required by the check criterion. Source documentation for these regulatory claims appears in source_basis arrays using abbreviated file-name references without full docs/tier1_normative_framework/extracted/ path prefixes. Representative examples: (1) governance_matrix[0].source_basis: 'governance_principles.json: no_prescribed_governance_body_names = true — body name General Assembly is a project-operational choice (programme-guide_horizon_en.pdf p.6; wp-15-general-annexes_horizon-2026-2027_en.pdf pp.3-4)' — the reference 'governance_principles.json' lacks the full docs/tier1_normative_framework/extracted/ prefix required to confirm Tier 1 extracted file provenance. (2) ethics_assessment.ethics_grounding.self_assessment_required_source: 'implementation_constraints.json: ethics_self_assessment_required — wp-15-general-annexes_horizon-2026-2027_en.pdf p.16; programme-guide_horizon_en.pdf pp.25-28' — 'implementation_constraints.json' is abbreviated and not resolvable to a full Tier 1 extracted path without reading that directory. (3) ethics_grounding.gender_plan_grounding: 'implementation_constraints.json: gender_equality_plan_eligibility — public bodies, research organisations...(wp-15-general-annexes_horizon-2026-2027_en.pdf pp.18-19)' — same abbreviated path pattern. The source_basis structure with abbreviated paths does not satisfy the check criterion requiring traceability_footer.primary_sources[] entries pointing to full docs/tier1_normative_framework/extracted/ paths. This is assessed as a structural traceability format issue; the claims are substantively sourced but the required formal traceability structure is absent.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1-4
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable to artifact type: check 13.10 applies exclusively to Tier 5 artifacts (proposal_sections, assembled_drafts, review_packets, final_exports). The audited artifact has schema_id 'orch.phase6.implementation_architecture.v1', identifying it as a Tier 4 orchestration state record, not a Tier 5 deliverable.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable to artifact-level check: whether Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents were modified to reflect project assumptions cannot be determined by reading a phase output artifact. This check requires inspection of the source document files in docs/tier1_normative_framework/ and docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/, not the produced output.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not applicable to artifact-level check: whether CLAUDE.md is being treated as advisory or optional cannot be determined from reading artifact content. This prohibition is enforced through constitutional compliance of the overall execution process and agent behavior, not by artifact inspection.
